meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn how to architect SaaS pricing models for clients using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert guide.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap ends when you stop looking for products and start looking for pricing models. By building modular SaaS pricing engines, you provide immediate value to clients, turning abstract business concepts into revenue-generating machinery. Use Antigravity to scaffold the logic and Claude Code to deploy the architecture.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for ambition. You've been staring at the screen, waiting for the 'perfect' SaaS idea to strike, while your competitors are already shipping value to paying clients. The paralysis of choice is not a lack of creativity; it is a lack of a structural framework to turn problems into profitable software solutions.

We are shifting the paradigm. Instead of chasing elusive ideas, we will build a robust, scalable SaaS pricing engine—a foundational component that every client needs. By mastering the architecture of value exchange, you become an indispensable partner rather than just another freelancer. Let's build the engine that powers the next generation of SaaS.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Model Architecture
steps.description: Establish a polymorphic pricing structure that supports tiered subscriptions, usage-based billing, and flat-fee add-ons. This provides the backbone for any future SaaS project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript schema for a SaaS pricing engine that supports three tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise) with dynamic feature flags and usage-based billing logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create pricing-schema.ts based on the generated Antigravity model'

steps.title: Build the Revenue Logic Engine
steps.description: Implement the core business logic that calculates invoice amounts based on user activity and subscription status, ensuring auditability and precision.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust calculation engine in Node.js that handles pro-rated subscription updates and usage-based spikes for a multi-tenant SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement logic in revenue-engine.ts with unit tests'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Create the secure bridge between your pricing engine and payment gateways, ensuring that subscription state changes reflect instantly in your application database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Express.js middleware for Stripe webhooks that maps subscription.updated and invoice.payment_succeeded events to user account status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add webhook-handler.ts to the existing express server'

steps.title: Develop the Pricing UI Components
steps.description: Build high-conversion pricing tables that dynamically fetch data from your engine, allowing clients to change plans without deploying new code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React PricingTable component using Tailwind CSS that accepts a dynamic array of pricing tiers and highlights the 'Pro' plan by default.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate PricingTable.tsx with responsive design'

steps.title: Stress Test with Simulation Data
steps.description: Simulate thousands of subscription lifecycle events to ensure your pricing engine handles edge cases like mid-cycle downgrades without leaking revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that simulates 1,000 concurrent subscription lifecycle events to identify potential race conditions in the pricing engine.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run test:stress to validate pricing engine integrity'

pro_tips: Always separate your billing logic from your application logic; this allows you to swap payment providers without rewriting your entire backend.
Implement a 'grandfathered' pricing flag in your database schema from day one to avoid headaches when you inevitably raise your client's prices.
Use mock billing events in your development environment to test how your UI handles 'payment failed' states before showing them to real users.
Build a CLI tool for your clients that allows them to adjust pricing tiers without needing access to the production database.
Always log the raw request body of webhook events to a secure cloud bucket; it is your ultimate source of truth when a customer complains about an invoice.
content.next_steps: Package this pricing engine as a reusable micro-service and offer it as a 'Pricing-as-a-Service' module to your existing clients to generate recurring revenue.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build high-value pricing systems that solve the 'no project ideas' problem for good. By focusing on the infrastructure of revenue, you position yourself as a high-tier consultant. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
